meaning: adj.<俚>懦弱的,无用的,哭哭啼啼的
examples: Margaret Thatcher made no secret of her contempt for the wimpish men around her.
Despite a slightly wimpish image, Mr Dion, a former academic, certainly does not lack courage.
Margaret Thatcher made no secret of her contempt for the wimpish men around her. There is a joke about her going out to dinner with her cabinet.
American aficionados of rugby union can only dream of the Croesian sums of money that are lavished on American football, a sport that vaguely resembles rugby in wimpish armour-plating.
